headline: Meta adds paywall and 'rate limits' to smart glasses
summary: Meta's smart glasses now feature new 'rate limits'. Users face a $20 monthly fee for unlimited AI access. Conversation Focus is capped at three hours without subscription.

headline: Anthropic's long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is returning online globally. The AI was sidelined after weeks of Trump administration negotiations. Access will be restored across Claude, AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft.

headline: Dish files for bankruptcy, but not shutting down
summary: Dish, owner of Dish TV and Sling TV, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. This allows winding down wireless operations after 5G spectrum sale delays. Despite bankruptcy, Dish TV and Sling TV services will continue.

headline: Amazon fined $2.25 million for failing to help identity theft victims
summary: Amazon was fined $2.25 million by the FTC. The company failed to help customers who fell victim to identity theft. Amazon allegedly refused to provide details on fraudulent purchases.

headline: Reacher Season 4 Returns in August, Neagley Spin-Off Lands the Following Month
summary: Reacher Season 4 premieres on Prime Video in August. A spin-off series focusing on Neagley will launch the following month. Premiere dates are now set for the ex-MP MVPs.

headline: The New Rick & Morty GLD Jewelry Drop Is Here and the Pickle Rick Pendant Already Sold Out
summary: A new Rick & Morty GLD Jewelry collection has been released. The popular Pickle Rick pendant quickly sold out. Fans eagerly anticipated this new merchandise drop.

headline: Ex-PlayStation Boss Defends PC Strategy as Sony Pivots Away from Multiplatform Releases
summary: Former SIEA President Shawn Layden defended PlayStation's past PC strategy. This comes as Sony pivots away from multiplatform PC releases. Layden explained the rationale behind earlier decisions.

headline: Everything Coming to Amazon Prime Video in July
summary: Amazon Prime Video announces its content lineup for July. New movies, series, and shows will be added. Subscribers can look forward to fresh entertainment options.

headline: Starmer trims budgets to fund extra £15bn for defence
summary: Keir Starmer plans to boost UK defense spending by £15bn. Other departmental budgets will be trimmed to fund this increase. The plan details future spending on UK armed forces.

headline: Why Starmer's defence plan leaves next PM with £4.7bn headache
summary: Keir Starmer's defense plan includes significant spending increases. This creates a projected £4.7bn financial challenge. The issue will be a major concern for the incoming Prime Minister.

headline: Plea for households to read energy meter as prices rise
summary: Household energy prices have increased by 13% annually. Ofgem's latest price cap is now in effect. Consumers are urged to submit accurate meter readings.

headline: Why Gen Z are planning for life without a state pension
summary: Many younger people doubt the future existence of the state pension. Gen Z is actively planning for retirement without relying on it. This reflects a growing concern about future social security.
